commits.message:
mit-styling-aj1.7.6: the loupe — a uniform push into one specimen glyph

Recast the zoom node from a gradient Ken Burns frame into THE LOUPE: a uniform
push into one showpiece serif glyph (a double-story g, the glyph type designers
obsess over). One transform, one fixed origin — the whole stage magnifies through
a fixed loupe reticle — deliberately distinct from parallax's DIFFERENTIAL
multi-plane drift and from snow's falling particles.

The node's thesis ('the detail was always there') is made literal instead of
faked: hamburgefonstiv — the craft's own type-tester word — is set into the
glyph's counter from frame 0 at sub-pixel size and grows into legibility purely
by magnification, never conjured at a threshold [LAW:no-silent-failure].

One eased scale scalar drives BOTH the transform and the honest readout
(LOUPE xN.N - 18->N PT, the specimen size-scale climb) [LAW:one-source-of-truth],
so the number on screen can never disagree with the picture. The push is gated on
n.w (read-beat, beat:'dwell') so the glyph holds still at full size through the
opening beat before the descent — the horizontal-node precedent — which also
honors bmf's 'longer, double or triple it': tempo bumped phrase->movement (the
palette's longest breath; can't coin a longer one [LAW:no-mode-explosion]).

Wears the gallery's shared print-specimen frame (matte jade crop marks + tracked
mono catalogue label, POSITIVE inset for the full-bleed scene, no bloom). Readout
written textContent-direct, not setText (a per-frame counter would flicker the
fade — the frame-sequence/parallax lesson). Pure scroll-driven, no WAAPI.

Verified in Chrome across the full scroll range: zero console errors/warnings,
clean monotonic scrub x1 held -> x9, all four crop-marks rendering, fine print
resolving from texture to legible.

Closes mit-styling-aj1.7.6

mit-styling-aj1.7.5: the stereo specimen — parallax planes pulled out of register (#28)

Recast the parallax form-node from rainbow blobs on a purple starfield into a
STEREO SPECIMEN: typographic furniture from a specimen sheet (a backdrop serif
glyph, a tracked size-scale, a baseline rule, a point-size numeral, a swatch, a
showpiece ampersand) spread across depth planes. One scalar d (0 far -> 1 near)
per plane drives drift, scale, opacity and blur [LAW:one-source-of-truth]; only
drift moves on scroll, so the flat sheet pulls out of register -- near planes
outrun far ones, depth from the difference in speed.

- Wears the gallery's print-specimen frame: matte jade crop marks (8-strip pseudo,
  positive inset for the full-bleed scene, as in horizontal) + tracked mono
  catalogue label, no bloom.
- Honest live readout: the real depth SPREAD, 0 at rest -> 100% at full pull
  (textContent-direct, not setText, to avoid per-frame fade flicker).
- Run extended to the movement tempo per bmf feedback ('last longer / be more of it').
- Distinct mechanic: the only z-depth scene in the gallery -- differential drift,
  not a uniform push (zoom) and not falling particles (snow).

Verified in Chrome across the node's full scroll range: zero console errors,
planes hold in register at p=0 and spread to 100% by p=1.
